commit_msg: feat: implement episode pipeline with RepoStory baseline context
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Episode Construct
subtitle: He built a stage for the repository to perform its own tragedy.
when_where: 🕐 Still 9:05 AM. Still main. Still the same terminal, the same cold coffee. 🌃 But now he was reaching deeper into the architecture, laying track for something that would turn raw git history into narrative. The pipeline wasn't enough. He needed *episodes*—chapters, acts, a story that felt inevitable.
who_whom: 🎭 The episode pipeline entered the scene with RepoStory baseline context—a framework that could read a repository like a detective reads a crime scene. 📊 Every commit became a plot point. Every branch became a suspect's alibi. The baseline context was the foundation: here's what we know to be true. Everything else is what he's trying to hide.
what_why: 💡 He wasn't just compressing git history anymore; he was *composing* it. The episode pipeline took the haiku work and elevated it into something bigger—serialized confessions, each one feeding into the next, building toward a climax that felt chosen rather than discovered. 🧠 By establishing baseline context first, he created a framework where deviation became *meaningful*—where every outlier, every strange commit, every 3 AM push looked like a deliberate choice rather than a desperate act. 💀 The repository wasn't just being read; it was being *directed*. He was the playwright, and the codebase was his unwilling actor.
verdict: He didn't tell the story of the code; he engineered the code to tell his story.
